---
title: "*The Unauthorized Guide to Using Spreadsheets in Chemical Engineering* (Spreadsheet Techniques) Reference Page"
author: "DOFPro group"
format: html
html-math-method: mathjax
---
::: {.callout-important title="Still Under Construction!"}
This web page is just a bare outline. The complete details have not been filled in yet.
:::
# Intro to Using Spreadsheets in Chemical Engineering

Main title explanation

## The Unauthorized Guide to Using Spreadsheets in Chemical Engineering

Video 1 explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/5XMeQ6vWymA?si=CUrnajQV7rkgAmgv" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

## How to Have a Quickie with Your Data Part 1

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/4EfhlWNDQ48?si=Y4u8lUIBWluyVgtZ" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

## How to Have a Quickie with Your Data Part 2

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/dVmh0EdQT1U?si=0A6c_LvtbfwbxQ7I" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

## Solving Nonlinear Equations with Spreadsheets

Video  explanation

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube.com/embed/SaI2qdu1wSE?si=Gn3Q6EGuSdcoeUrU" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>
[Visuals](../../NotYetHere.qmd)

# Examples and Definitions

## Examples Links

- [Guide to Spreadsheets Template](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/GuideToSpreadsheets.ods)
- [Guide to Spreadsheets Solutions](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/SolutionGuideToSpreadsheets.ods)
- [Data Fitting (Quickie) Template](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/DataFittingTemplate.ods)
- [Data Fitting (Quickie) Solutions](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/DataFittingSolution.ods)
- [Nonlinear Roots Demo](https://dofprobucket1.s3.us-east-2.amazonaws.com/calculations/NonlinearRootDemoForPosting.ods)

## Definitions

Spreadsheet
: A spreadsheet is a software application that allows users to organize, analyze, and manipulate data in a tabular format. It typically consists of a grid of cells arranged in rows and columns, where each cell can contain text, numbers, formulas, or functions. Spreadsheets are commonly used for tasks such as data entry, calculations, financial analysis, and creating charts or graphs.

Cell
: A cell is the basic unit of a spreadsheet, where data can be entered or calculated. Each cell is identified by a unique combination of a column letter and a row number (e.g., A1, B2). Cells can contain various types of data, including text, numbers, formulas, or functions. Formulas in cells can perform calculations based on the values of other cells, allowing for dynamic data analysis and manipulation.

Reference
: In the context of spreadsheets, a reference is a way to identify and access the value or formula contained in a specific cell or range of cells. References can be relative (e.g., A1), absolute (e.g., $A$1), or mixed (e.g., A$1). Relative references change when copied to another location, while absolute references remain constant. References are essential for performing calculations and creating formulas that depend on the values of other cells in the spreadsheet.

Function
: A function in a spreadsheet is a predefined formula that performs a specific calculation or operation on a set of values or arguments. Functions can be used to simplify complex calculations and automate tasks. Common functions include SUM (to add a range of numbers), AVERAGE (to calculate the mean), IF (to perform conditional logic), and VLOOKUP (to search for a value in a table).

Drag-Fill
: Drag-fill is a feature in spreadsheets that allows users to quickly copy and fill a series of values or formulas across a range of cells. By clicking and dragging the fill handle (a small square at the bottom-right corner of a selected cell), users can extend a pattern or sequence of data, such as numbers, dates, or formulas, to adjacent cells. This feature is particularly useful for creating repetitive data or applying the same formula to multiple cells without manually entering it each time.


# Other Links and Videos

## Other Links

## Previous and Following Videos

### The video previous to the previous video (i.e., two videos back)

**Interpol Is Late to a Mind-Blowing Math Hack, Just the Facts**<br/>*Linear Interpolation*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/VbnpWj9iYAU), [Info Page](../../basics/calculations/InterpolationWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../basics/calculations/InterpolationJTFVisuals.qmd)

### The previous video (i.e., one video back)

**No Clear Interpretation a Mind-Blowing Math Hack**<br/>*Nonlinear Interpolation*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/721FxKwDRAU), [Info Page](../../basics/calculations/InterpolationWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../basics/calculations/NLInterpolationJTFVisuals.qmd)

### The next video (i.e., one video forward)

**How Deviant and Mean Are Your Data? Intro and Basics Part 1, Just the Facts**<br/>*Mean ($\mu,\ \bar{x}$) and Standard Deviation ($\sigma,\ S$)*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/ksg4Babav6o), [Info Page](../../basics/stats/BasicStatisticsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../basics/stats/5-JTF-WhyStatsJTF1Visuals.qmd)

### The next after next video (i.e. two videos forward)

**How Deviant and Mean Are Your Data? Part 2**<br/>*Standard Error ($S_{\bar{x}}$) and Confidence Interval ($2\lambda$)*<br/>[Video](https://youtu.be/cBOrE6eV-AE), [Info Page](../../basics/stats/BasicStatisticsVideoInfoWebPage.qmd), [Visuals](../../basics/stats/5A-JTF-WhyStatsJTF2Visuals.qmd)
